title: [politoed] Battle Guide
summary: A [water]-type support that triggers [drizzle] for a rain team, backed by utility moves like [encore], [toxic], and [scald].
strengths: [drizzle] sets up permanent rain on entry
75/100 dual bulk enables tank duties alongside support
[encore] plus [toxic] locks and chips opposing setup attackers
weaknesses: Grass and Electric weaknesses threaten from fast special attackers
Low offensive stats mean minimal direct pressure
taunt completely shuts down the utility plan

recommendedMoves.reason: [water] STAB with burn chance against physical attackers
Locks an opposing Pokemon into its last move
Inflicts ongoing damage on opposing walls
Stalls for passive damage and scouts switches

recommendedItems.reason: Wakes up immediately after a rest cycle

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[politoed] is a [water]-type support Pokemon whose entire role is built around the hidden ability [drizzle].5x and halving Fire-type damage taken. In the Champions format, [politoed] is the weather setter for dedicated rain teams, and its 90/75/100 defensive line gives it enough bulk to survive long enough to support with utility moves like [encore], [toxic], and [protect].

## Strengths
[drizzle] is the primary reason to run [politoed]. The rain weather is a system-level buff that changes the damage calculation for every [water] attacker on the team, and simultaneously reduces incoming Fire damage by half, which creates a structural advantage across the entire match. Beyond [drizzle], [politoed]'s utility moveset ([scald] for burns, [encore] for lock-ins, [toxic] for passive damage, [protect] for stall) makes it a flexible slot that can stall out opposing walls while the [water] attackers on the back line clean up.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 2x Grass and Electric weaknesses are exploited by almost every fast special attacker in the format, and [politoed] is too slow to outspeed the threats that would remove it. Offensively [politoed] is almost passive. [politoed] is also only valuable on dedicated rain teams, which limits its slot flexibility significantly.

## Recommended Moveset
[scald] is the locked [water] STAB because its 30 percent burn chance is a meaningful threat to physical attackers. [encore] is the utility slot for locking opposing Pokemon into setup moves or weak attacks. [toxic] covers the passive damage plan on opposing walls that would otherwise outlast [politoed]. [protect] fills the fourth slot to stall for [toxic] damage and scout switches. ice-beam is an alternate coverage pick for Grass and Dragon attackers.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default for passive recovery that stacks with the rain team plan. [chesto-berry] is an alternate for rest-based builds that want a one-turn full heal. The ability must be [drizzle]. Tera [water] maxes STAB damage, and Tera [fairy] flips Dragon and Dark attacks into resists.

## Teammates & Synergy
[politoed] is the core of a dedicated rain team. Grass and Electric answers are mandatory (Ground or Fire partners cover the main threats), and a cleaner on the back line converts the rain-boosted damage into wins. A weather counter on the opposing side can neutralize [politoed]'s utility entirely, so be prepared to set rain again mid-match.

## Play Patterns
Lead [politoed] to set rain, then start the utility rotation. Pivot out when Grass or Electric attackers are incoming, and save [politoed]'s HP for a second rain activation later in the match. Save the Tera for an Electric or Grass move that would otherwise KO [politoed], using it defensively to preserve the rain setter slot.
